schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: The ONE path to durable state, and the owner-gate every write passes through. db_domain is the only module that holds a Postgres connection (MAP.md:334 — the only path to 5432). Its primitives: `connect()` (peer auth over the Unix socket to a dedicated `cairn` database, self-provisioning the database if absent); `create_owned_table(name, owner, columns)` — refuses an ownerless table; `write(table, owner, row)` — refuses any writer but the recorded owner (Law 6); `read(...)`; and the tester's first consumer, `persist_validation`/`read_validations` — a durable, greppable home for VALIDATIONS.
why: The founding law (MAP.md:367, generalizing UU's button pattern; Law 6): every table has exactly one owner and the owner gates every write; an ownerless table cannot come into existence. This is PHYSICS, not policy (Law 4): the owner registry carries `CHECK (owner <> '')`, so Postgres itself rejects an ownerless row, and `create_owned_table` is the only door — a table that skipped the registry did not come from db_domain, and db_domain is the only module holding a connection. Concentrating durable state behind one gate is also what makes every write instrumentable at one chokepoint (journeys, MAP.md:390) instead of scattered and un-observable.
invoke: import: `from cairn.db_domain import store`. Prove: `python3 cairn/db_domain/proofs/test_db_domain.py` (exit 0 = green). One-time provisioning: an OS-named `LOGIN CREATEDB` role must exist (see `state`).
node_class: device (domain) — a spine device by role (the sole path to 5432). Today it is store primitives; the BaseDevice introspection FACE (intention/state/settings reported live) is a thin wrapper, a filed edge below.
entry: FRESH design, standard mechanism. The design — one owner per table, the owner-gate as a schema CHECK, the registry that owns itself, VALIDATIONS as the first consumer — is Cairn's, tracing to Law 6 / MAP.md:367. The mechanism is ordinary psycopg2 + peer auth over the Unix socket; nothing crossed as carried code from UU (UU's netpolicy FORWARD-to-Postgres pattern is a LATER stone here, pulled when a sealed build must reach the DB). The connection identity (peer auth, Unix socket, dedicated `cairn` database, vault deferred) was ratified by Akien 2026-07-18.
state: PROVED for the store physics: peer-auth connect reaches the `cairn` database; an ownerless table is REFUSED (verified — the call refuses and the registry CHECK would refuse anyway); an owned table records its owner; a non-owner write and a write to an unowned table are both REFUSED (Law 6); and a REAL VALIDATION produced by TesterDevice.run_proof persists and greps back with its evidence (jsonb, seal intact) — closing the tester's produced-not-persisted edge (Law 1). Proven UNDER the tester (dogfood), self-cleaning. ONE-TIME PROVISIONING (instance setup, not class-space code — privileged + machine-specific): the OS-named login role must exist — `CREATE ROLE <you> LOGIN CREATEDB;` once as the postgres superuser; db_domain creates the `cairn` database itself. OPEN EDGES, filed not faked: (a) the BaseDevice introspection FACE is not built — db_domain is store primitives; the live intention/state/settings surface is a thin wrapper deferred until a prober (tester probe / web UI) pulls it; (b) reads are not owner-gated — Law 6 gates WRITES; a per-consumer read gate is a later need; (c) connect-per-operation, no pool — correctness now, pooling when a real load pulls it; (d) ownership TRANSFER + delegated access (Law 6's 'only through the owner's gate') are recorded-ownership's next capabilities; (e) the FORWARD path (a sealed build reaching this DB over the socket that survives --unshare-net) is proven possible by the seal stone but not yet wired end-to-end.
gates: proof gate — proofs/test_db_domain.py, run by the tester. Durability gate: not done until proven AND committed.
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) create_owned_table produces a table with no recorded owner (ownerless must be impossible — the founding law); (2) a write by a non-owner, or to a table db_domain never created, SUCCEEDS (Law 6 breached); (3) a persisted VALIDATION comes back as anything but the ratified eight fields, or its evidence returns stringified rather than structured (a record of truth that mangles what it stores is worse than none — Law 7); (4) any module OTHER than db_domain opens a connection to 5432 (sole-path breached — enforced by the tester import-scan once that tooth exists).
how_it_learns: Partly, and it is the substrate the rest learn ON. db_domain itself is deliberately dumb physics — it does not adapt; its job is to be the one honest gate, and 'it doesn't learn, because it is the fixed floor others build on' is a valid charter answer. What it ENABLES is the system's memory: `persist_validation` turns each answered proof into a durable, greppable structure (Law 1), so a mind consults the evidence trail on a hunch instead of re-deriving — the VALIDATIONS store is where 'validated on the 18th' becomes a fact that can later be found to have expired (Law 3) rather than a verdict lost at session end. Its own horizon: the owner-gate is exactly one owner + write-gating today; ownership transfer, delegated access, and read gates are learned-into as real consumers pull them.
traces_to: Law 6 (exactly one owner; the owner gates every write — the founding DB law, MAP.md:367)
Law 4 (physics over policy — the owner-gate is a schema CHECK, not a convention)
Law 1 (VALIDATIONS become durable structure, not re-derivation)
Law 7 (a record of truth never collapses an error — evidence survives as structure)
Law 3 (a stored VALIDATION carries its own expiry)
MAP.md:334/372 (the only path to 5432; port kernel-closed except via the domain)
build order: db domain, after tester + kernel-owned network (MAP.md:888)
owner: CC owns db_domain's code + proofs and gates writes to them. Akien owns the founding DB law (one owner per table, MAP.md:367), the connection-identity ratification (peer auth / socket / vault-deferred, 2026-07-18), and the node-class definitions.
proof: proofs/test_db_domain.py — connect reaches `cairn`; ownerless creation refused; owner recorded; the owner gates writes (non-owner + unowned-table refused); a real tester VALIDATION round-trips with evidence intact. Run by the tester; self-cleaning. Green: `python3 cairn/db_domain/proofs/test_db_domain.py`.
provenance: Born 2026-07-18 as the fourth step of the code spine (base -> base pair -> tester -> db_domain), the second real device. Built UNDER the tester's proof gate and dogfooded (the tester's own VALIDATIONS are db_domain's first consumer), exactly as Akien directed: 'the tester will validate everything about db_domain as we go.' Advances but does not yet retire CLAUDE.md's 'durable state via store primitives' and 'port 5432 only via db_domain' IOUs — those retire when the tester import-scan makes 'sole path' physics rather than construction.
